title: From Sets to Relations via Cartesian Product
description: A visual explanation of how Cartesian products form the foundation for defining relations between sets.
elements: Relation R (as a subset of A × B)
relationships: Set A and Set B are input sets
Cartesian Product A × B is formed from all possible ordered pairs
A Relation R is defined as any subset of the Cartesian product A × B
An Ordered Pair (a, b) is a single element of the Cartesian product and may be part of relation R
